seo.title: RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization in OpenAI Agents SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix rag retrieval quality optimization in OpenAI Agents SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In OpenAI Agents SDK, RAG Retrieval Quality issues manifest as agents receiving irrelevant or incomplete context from vector stores during tool calls, leading to hallucinated responses and infinite handoff loops between specialized agents. Poor retrieval quality causes the Runner to pass noisy context to agent.run() calls, bypassing guardrails and breaking multi-agent workflows. This is exacerbated by the SDK's reliance on external RAG tools without built-in relevance validation.

howItManifests.scenario: Research agent retrieves outdated docs for financial query
howItManifests.symptom: Agent returns 2022 pricing data for 2024 product inquiry
howItManifests.impact: Handoff to billing agent fails, creates infinite correction loop

howItManifests.scenario: Customer support agent gets irrelevant code snippets
howItManifests.symptom: Python error response contains JavaScript examples
howItManifests.impact: Guardrail flags hallucination, blocks response to user

howItManifests.scenario: Legal agent retrieves wrong jurisdiction documents
howItManifests.symptom: EU GDPR docs returned for US HIPAA compliance query
howItManifests.impact: Multi-agent workflow stalls at validation handoff

howItManifests.scenario: Code agent gets deprecated API references
howItManifests.symptom: Generates code using removed OpenAI Agents SDK v0.1 methods
howItManifests.impact: Tracing shows repeated tool failures across agent chain

detection.name: Semantic Similarity Check
detection.description: Compare retrieved context semantic similarity to original query using embeddings
detection.code:
from openai import OpenAI
from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity
import numpy as np

client = OpenAI()

def detect_retrieval_quality(query: str, retrieved_docs: list[str], threshold: float = 0.7) -> dict:
    query_embedding = client.embeddings.create(
        input=query, model="text-embedding-3-small"
    ).data[0].embedding
    
    doc_embeddings = [
        client.embeddings.create(input=doc, model="text-embedding-3-small")
        .data[0].embedding
        for doc in retrieved_docs
    ]
    
    similarities = cosine_similarity([query_embedding], doc_embeddings)[0]
    avg_similarity = np.mean(similarities)
    
    return {
        'is_quality': avg_similarity > threshold,
        'avg_similarity': avg_similarity,
        'threshold': threshold
    }

detection.name: Keyword Overlap Validation
detection.description: Ensure critical query keywords appear in retrieved context
detection.code:
import re
from collections import Counter

def keyword_overlap_check(query: str, retrieved_docs: list[str]) -> dict:
    query_words = re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', query.lower())
    query_counter = Counter(query_words)
    
    doc_text = ' '.join(retrieved_docs).lower()
    doc_words = re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', doc_text)
    doc_counter = Counter(doc_words)
    
    overlap_score = sum(min(query_counter[word], doc_counter[word]) 
                       for word in query_counter) / len(query_words)
    
    return {
        'overlap_score': overlap_score,
        'is_relevant': overlap_score > 0.3,
        'missing_keywords': [w for w in query_words if doc_counter[w] == 0]
    }

detection.name: Agent Context Length Monitor
detection.description: Detect when RAG floods agent context exceeding token limits

fixes.name: Hybrid Retrieval with Re-ranking
fixes.description: Combine keyword + semantic search with Cohere reranker
fixes.code:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

research_agent = Agent(
    name="ResearchAgent",
    instructions="You are a research specialist...",
    tools=[rag_tool]
)

@VexGuardrail(name="rag_quality")
def validate_rag_retrieval(query: str, docs: list[str]) -> bool:
    # Hybrid scoring
    semantic_score = detect_retrieval_quality(query, docs)['avg_similarity']
    keyword_score = keyword_overlap_check(query, docs)['overlap_score']
    return (semantic_score > 0.65) and (keyword_score > 0.25)

# Apply guardrail to agent tool calls
research_agent.add_guardrail(validate_rag_retrieval)

runner = Runner([research_agent])

fixes.name: Dynamic Context Truncation
fixes.description: Smart truncation preserving most relevant chunks

fixes.name: Fallback Retrieval Strategy
fixes.description: Multi-index fallback when primary retrieval fails quality checks
fixes.code:
def robust_rag_retrieval(query: str) -> list[str]:
    # Primary vector search
    primary_docs = vector_search(query, k=5)
    
    if not validate_rag_retrieval(query, primary_docs):
        print("Primary retrieval failed quality check, trying fallback")
        
        # Fallback 1: Keyword search
        keyword_docs = keyword_search(query, k=5)
        if validate_rag_retrieval(query, keyword_docs):
            return keyword_docs
        
        # Fallback 2: Hybrid search
        hybrid_docs = hybrid_search(query, k=5)
        return hybrid_docs
    
    return primary_docs

# Integrate with agent tool
rag_tool = Tool(
    name="robust_rag",
    description="Reliable RAG retrieval with quality fallbacks",
    function=robust_rag_retrieval
)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade RAG quality monitoring as output guardrails for OpenAI Agents SDK workflows. Deploy VexGuardrail instances on each agent's tool outputs to automatically catch low-relevance retrievals before they propagate through multi-agent handoffs. Vex's ML-powered detectors analyze semantic similarity, keyword coverage, and context overflow in real-time, blocking bad retrievals and triggering fallback strategies.

Vex traces the full retrieval → agent → handoff chain, pinpointing exactly which vector index or embedding model is causing quality degradation. Automated remediation includes dynamic re-ranking, index switching, and query rewriting. Vex dashboards show retrieval quality trends across all agents, enabling proactive index maintenance and embedding optimization.

Integration takes <5 minutes: pip install vex-sdk, wrap your agents with VexGuardrail, and get instant RAG quality scores in your traces.
vexAutomation.code:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail, Vex

# Initialize Vex
vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-key")

# Define RAG quality guardrail
@vex.guardrail(name="rag-retrieval-quality")
def rag_quality_guard(context: dict) -> dict:
    query = context.get('query', '')
    docs = context.get('retrieved_docs', [])
    
    # Run all detectors
    semantic = detect_retrieval_quality(query, docs)
    keywords = keyword_overlap_check(query, docs)
    length = monitor_context_length(query, docs)
    
    score = (semantic['avg_similarity'] * 0.5 + 
             keywords['overlap_score'] * 0.3 + 
             (1 if not length['is_overflow'] else 0) * 0.2)
    
    return {
        'pass': score > 0.7,
        'score': score,
        'metrics': {'semantic': semantic, 'keywords': keywords, 'length': length},
        'remediation': 're-rank' if score < 0.7 else None
    }

# Production agent workflow
research_agent = Agent(
    name="Research",
    tools=[rag_tool],
    output_guardrails=[rag_quality_guard]
)

billing_agent = Agent(
    name="Billing",
    input_guardrails=[rag_quality_guard]
)

# Vex traces everything automatically
runner = Runner([research_agent, billing_agent])
result = runner.run("What's current pricing for Enterprise plan?")

# View traces: https://app.vex.dev/project/YOUR_PROJECT/traces
